description: Address review findings: (1) Feature flags not gating feature rendering - wire them to components, (2) SettingsContext stale closure - fix the setter pattern, (3) Games creating unmanaged Audio elements - pause main player when game starts, (4) Crossfade interval orphaning Audio elements - extract shared helper and track fading elements.
steps: 1. Fix SettingsContext stale closure: change all useCallback setters to read currentSettings at call time instead of capturing it in closure. Since currentSettings is a module-level variable, remove empty dependency arrays or use a getter pattern.
2. Fix feature flags: create a FeatureFlagGate wrapper component that reads feature flags and conditionally renders children. Wrap flagged components in layout.tsx with the gate.
3. Fix games creating unmanaged Audio: import useAudioPlayer in GuessTheSong and pause the main player before starting game audio.
4. Fix crossfade interval orphaning Audio elements: extract crossfade logic into a shared helper function, track the fading-out element separately.
acceptance_criteria: SettingsContext setters always read fresh currentSettings at call time
Feature-flagged components in layout.tsx are gated by their respective flags
GuessTheSong pauses the main audio player before playing game audio
Crossfade logic is extracted into a single helper, fading-out elements are tracked separately
bun run lint passes with zero errors
bun run build succeeds with zero errors
verification: bun run lint passes with zero errors
bun run build succeeds with zero errors
findings: All four priority review issues addressed. (1) Feature flags now gate 7 components in layout.tsx via FeatureFlagGate that uses useSyncExternalStore and custom events. (2) SettingsContext setters now explicitly call getSettingsSnapshot() to ensure fresh module-level state is read. (3) GuessTheSong imports useAudioPlayer and pauses main player before playing game audio. (4) Crossfade logic extracted into performCrossfade/transitionAudio helpers with fadingOutRef tracking fading elements to prevent orphaning. MemoryGame does not actually use Audio (only visual matching). Build and lint pass cleanly with zero errors and zero warnings.
